name: ENTITY-MIB Hardware Inventory
description: ENTITY-MIB hardware inventory collector. Walks entPhysicalTable to capture chassis, modules, ports, transceivers, fans, and PSUs across any standards-compliant SNMP device.
author: MonCTL
changelog: v1.0.0 - Bundles entity_mib_inventory 1.0.5 plus the 8 ENTITY-MIB OIDs (entPhysicalDescr, entPhysicalClass, entPhysicalName, entPhysicalHardwareRev, etc.). Requires built-in SNMP connector.

apps.name: entity_mib_inventory
apps.description: ENTITY-MIB hardware inventory collector — discovers chassis components, modules, PSUs, fans, and optics via SNMP. Requires SNMP connector bound with alias 'snmp'.

include_ports.title: Include Ports
include_ports.description: Include physical port entries in inventory

include_sensors.title: Include Sensors
include_sensors.description: Include temperature, voltage, current, and power sensors in inventory

versions.source_code:
"""ENTITY-MIB hardware inventory collector.

Walks ENTITY-MIB entPhysicalTable to discover chassis components, modules,
power supplies, fans, and pluggable optics. Stores structured inventory data
in the config table.

Works with any device supporting ENTITY-MIB (RFC 6933): Cisco IOS-XR/IOS-XE/NX-OS,
Juniper, Arista, Nokia, etc.

v1.0.3: Use snmp.walk_multi() to fetch all 8 columns in a single GETBULK
        request stream instead of 8 sequential single-column walks. Same
        load on the agent (one in-flight request at a time) but ~8× fewer
        round-trips, which prevents 30s timeouts on large chassis.
        Requires SNMP connector ≥ 1.4.0.
v1.0.4: Emit config_data keys in the `{type}::{component}::{attr}` form
        expected by the pack's display_template and by the frontend's
        ConfigDataRenderer fallback (both group by splitting on `::`).
        Summary keys are underscore-prefixed (`_entity_mib_supported`,
        `_component_count`) so the template's grouping loop treats them
        as metadata instead of component rows. v1.0.3 was silently
        rendering as empty in the UI because the previous flat
        `{component}.attr` format didn't match the template's parser.
v1.0.5: Drop the AttributeError fallback to sequential walks — SNMP
        connector v1.4.2 (is_latest in prod 2026-04-20) now ships a real
        walk_multi. The fallback path was silently hot-running on every
        poll because v1.4.1 was byte-identical to the 1.3.0 seed and
        never had walk_multi. Also add dense logger.debug tracing around
        walk_multi for Debug Run visibility.

Connector binding required:
    alias "snmp" -> SNMP connector with credential providing auth parameters

Config options:
    include_sensors  bool   Include sensor entries (default: false)
    include_ports    bool   Include port entries (default: false)

Output config_data keys per component (colon-colon separated):
    {type}::{component}::model              - model / part number
    {type}::{component}::serial_number      - serial number
    {type}::{component}::manufacturer       - manufacturer name
    {type}::{component}::description        - human-readable description
    {type}::{component}::hardware_revision  - hardware revision
    {type}::{component}::firmware_revision  - firmware revision

Summary keys (underscore-prefixed):
    _entity_mib_supported  - "true" if entPhysicalTable returned rows
    _component_count       - total components after filtering
"""

import logging
import time

from monctl_collector.polling.base import BasePoller
from monctl_collector.jobs.models import PollContext, PollResult

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


# ENTITY-MIB OIDs (entPhysicalTable)
_ENT_PHYSICAL_BASE = "1.3.6.1.2.1.47.1.1.1.1"
_OIDS = {
    "descr":        f"{_ENT_PHYSICAL_BASE}.2",
    "class":        f"{_ENT_PHYSICAL_BASE}.5",
    "name":         f"{_ENT_PHYSICAL_BASE}.7",
    "hardware_rev": f"{_ENT_PHYSICAL_BASE}.8",
    "firmware_rev": f"{_ENT_PHYSICAL_BASE}.9",
    "serial":       f"{_ENT_PHYSICAL_BASE}.11",
    "manufacturer": f"{_ENT_PHYSICAL_BASE}.12",
    "model":        f"{_ENT_PHYSICAL_BASE}.13",
}

# entPhysicalClass -> component_type mapping
_CLASS_MAP = {
    1: "other",
    3: "chassis",
    6: "psu",
    7: "fan",
    8: "sensor",
    9: "module",
    10: "port",
    11: "chassis",
    12: "module",
}

# Classes to always skip
_SKIP_CLASSES = {2, 4, 5}  # unknown, backplane, container

# Description patterns indicating non-physical sub-components
_SKIP_DESCR_PATTERNS = [
    "FPD Module",
]


def _clean_str(val):
    """Strip and normalize SNMP string values."""
    if not val:
        return ""
    s = str(val).strip().strip('"').strip()
    if s in ("", "N/A",
             "No Such Instance currently exists at this OID",
             "No Such Object available on this agent at this OID"):
        return ""
    return s


def _extract_index(full_oid, base_oid):
    """Extract the entPhysicalIndex from the full OID."""
    suffix = full_oid[len(base_oid):]
    if suffix.startswith("."):
        suffix = suffix[1:]
    try:
        return int(suffix)
    except (ValueError, TypeError):
        return None


def _safe_key(name):
    """Make a component name safe for use as a config_key segment.

    The display template placeholder regex allows: [a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_.-]*
    So we replace slashes and spaces, and ensure it starts with a letter.
    """
    s = name.replace("/", "_").replace(" ", "_").replace("::", "_")
    # Remove any chars that aren't alphanumeric, underscore, dot, or hyphen
    s = "".join(c for c in s if c.isalnum() or c in "_.-")
    # Ensure starts with a letter (prefix with 'c.' if starts with digit)
    if s and not s[0].isalpha() and s[0] != "_":
        s = "c." + s
    return s


class Poller(BasePoller):
    """ENTITY-MIB hardware inventory poller."""

    async def poll(self, context: PollContext) -> PollResult:
        start = time.time()
        include_sensors = context.parameters.get("include_sensors", False)
        include_ports = context.parameters.get("include_ports", False)

        snmp = context.connectors.get("snmp")
        if snmp is None:
            return PollResult(
                job_id=context.job.job_id,
                device_id=context.job.device_id,
                collector_node=context.node_id,
                timestamp=time.time(),
                metrics=[], config_data=None,
                status="error", reachable=False,
                error_message="No SNMP connector bound (expected alias 'snmp'). "
                              "Go to Apps -> entity_mib_inventory and bind the SNMP connector with alias 'snmp'.",
                error_category="config",
                execution_time_ms=0,
            )

        try:
            # Walk all 8 columns in a single correlated GETBULK stream.
            # SNMP connector v1.4.2+ exposes walk_multi — required.
            logger.debug("entity_mib_inventory: walk_multi over %d columns",
                         len(_OIDS))
            per_oid = await snmp.walk_multi(list(_OIDS.values()))
            for base_oid, rows in per_oid.items():
                logger.debug("  %s: %d rows", base_oid, len(rows))

            raw = {col_name: {} for col_name in _OIDS}
            for col_name, base_oid in _OIDS.items():
                col_data = {}
                for full_oid, val in per_oid.get(base_oid, []):
                    idx = _extract_index(full_oid, base_oid)
                    if idx is not None:
                        col_data[idx] = str(val) if val is not None else ""
                raw[col_name] = col_data

            # Get all discovered entity indices
            all_indices = set()
            for col_data in raw.values():
                all_indices.update(col_data.keys())

            if not all_indices:
                # Device is reachable via SNMP but entPhysicalTable is empty
                # or unsupported. Signal this as an app-level error so the
                # Checks tab shows a DEVICE chip and a clear message, not a
                # silent CRITICAL row with no output.
                execution_ms = int((time.time() - start) * 1000)
                return PollResult(
                    job_id=context.job.job_id,
                    device_id=context.job.device_id,
                    collector_node=context.node_id,
                    timestamp=time.time(),
                    metrics=[{"name": "entity_count", "value": 0}],
                    config_data={"_entity_mib_supported": "false"},
                    status="error",
                    reachable=True,
                    error_message=(
                        "ENTITY-MIB not supported on this device "
                        "(entPhysicalTable walk returned no rows)."
                    ),
                    error_category="device",
                    execution_time_ms=execution_ms,
                )

            # Build entity records, filter, and produce config_data
            config_data = {}
            seen_serials = {}  # serial -> lowest entPhysicalIndex
            component_count = 0

            for idx in sorted(all_indices):
                ent_class = 0
                try:
                    ent_class = int(raw.get("class", {}).get(idx, 0) or 0)
                except (ValueError, TypeError):
                    pass

                descr = _clean_str(raw.get("descr", {}).get(idx, ""))
                name = _clean_str(raw.get("name", {}).get(idx, ""))
                serial = _clean_str(raw.get("serial", {}).get(idx, ""))
                model = _clean_str(raw.get("model", {}).get(idx, ""))
                manufacturer = _clean_str(raw.get("manufacturer", {}).get(idx, ""))
                hw_rev = _clean_str(raw.get("hardware_rev", {}).get(idx, ""))
                fw_rev = _clean_str(raw.get("firmware_rev", {}).get(idx, ""))

                # Skip unwanted classes
                if ent_class in _SKIP_CLASSES:
                    continue

                # Map class to component_type
                component_type = _CLASS_MAP.get(ent_class, "other")

                # Skip sensors unless explicitly included
                if component_type == "sensor" and not include_sensors:
                    continue

                # Skip ports unless explicitly included
                if component_type == "port" and not include_ports:
                    continue

                # Skip FPD and similar non-physical sub-components
                skip = False
                for pattern in _SKIP_DESCR_PATTERNS:
                    if pattern in descr:
                        skip = True
                        break
                if skip:
                    continue

                # Skip entries with no serial AND no model
                if not serial and not model:
                    continue

                # Deduplicate by serial (keep lowest index = primary entity)
                if serial:
                    if serial in seen_serials:
                        continue
                    seen_serials[serial] = idx

                # Build component key from name
                component = name or descr or f"entity-{idx}"
                key_prefix = _safe_key(component)

                component_count += 1

                # Emit keys in the `{component_type}::{component}::{attr}`
                # format expected by both the pack's display_template and
                # the frontend's ConfigDataRenderer fallback (which groups
                # by splitting on `::`). Component type is encoded in the
                # key prefix, so no separate `.type` field is needed.
                # Attribute names match the template's attrOrder.
                base = f"{component_type}::{key_prefix}"
                if model:
                    config_data[f"{base}::model"] = model
                if serial:
                    config_data[f"{base}::serial_number"] = serial
                if manufacturer:
                    config_data[f"{base}::manufacturer"] = manufacturer
                if descr:
                    config_data[f"{base}::description"] = descr
                if hw_rev:
                    config_data[f"{base}::hardware_revision"] = hw_rev
                if fw_rev:
                    config_data[f"{base}::firmware_revision"] = fw_rev

            # Summary keys — underscore-prefixed so the template's
            # grouping loop recognises them as non-component metadata.
            config_data["_entity_mib_supported"] = "true"
            config_data["_component_count"] = str(component_count)

            execution_ms = int((time.time() - start) * 1000)

            return PollResult(
                job_id=context.job.job_id,
                device_id=context.job.device_id,
                collector_node=context.node_id,
                timestamp=time.time(),
                metrics=[
                    {"name": "entity_count", "value": float(component_count)},
                ],
                config_data=config_data,
                status="ok",
                reachable=True,
                error_message=None,
                execution_time_ms=execution_ms,
            )

        except Exception as exc:
            execution_ms = int((time.time() - start) * 1000)
            return PollResult(
                job_id=context.job.job_id,
                device_id=context.job.device_id,
                collector_node=context.node_id,
                timestamp=time.time(),
                metrics=[], config_data=None,
                status="critical", reachable=False,
                error_message=f"{type(exc).__name__}: {exc}",
                error_category="device",
                execution_time_ms=execution_ms,
            )

snmp_oids.name: entPhysicalDescr
snmp_oids.description: ENTITY-MIB: Human-readable description of the physical entity

snmp_oids.name: entPhysicalSerialNum
snmp_oids.description: ENTITY-MIB: Serial number of the physical entity
